etymology.description: semantic 目(目) (mù, "eye") + phonetic 艮(艮) (gèn) -> meaning
etymology.interpretation: The character 眼(yǎn, "eye") combines 目(mù, "eye") as the semantic component, indicating the meaning related to the eyes. 艮(gèn) serves as the phonetic component, contributing the sound but not the meaning.

examples.chinese: 她有一双漂亮的眼睛(她有一雙漂亮的眼睛)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǒu yī shuāng piàoliang de yǎnjīng.
examples.english: She has a pair of beautiful eyes.

examples.chinese: 在黑暗中(在黑暗中)，我看见了一束光(我看見了一束光)。
examples.pinyin: Zài hēi'àn zhōng, wǒ kànjiànle yī shù guāng.
examples.english: In the darkness, I saw a beam of light.

examples.chinese: 他的眼神流露出悲伤(他的眼神流露出悲傷)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yǎnshén liúlù chū bēishāng.
